name: Design Brief Completeness Gate
description: Asserts .agentics/BRIEF.md exists and contains all 11 required sections (Industry, Mission, Vision, Values, Positioning, Target audience, Primary persona, Top 3 user goals, Top 3 user pain points, Product type, Primary platform). The strategic brief is the input to /aesthetic-fanout — if it's thin or missing, the resulting aesthetic lock is structurally weak.

args: F=.agentics/BRIEF.md; test -f "$F" || { echo '{"summary":{"errors":1},"findings":[{"severity":"error","path":"$F","message":"BRIEF.md missing — Station 1 did not run /design-brief gather. Strategic brief is the required input to /aesthetic-fanout."}]}'; exit 1; }; missing=(); for h in 'Industry' 'Mission' 'Vision' 'Values' 'Positioning' 'Target audience' 'Primary persona' 'Top 3 user goals' 'Top 3 user pain points' 'Product type' 'Primary platform'; do grep -F "## $h" "$F" >/dev/null || missing+=("$h"); done; if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then printf '{"summary":{"errors":%d},"findings":[' ${#missing[@]}; first=1; for m in "${missing[@]}"; do [ $first -eq 1 ] || printf ','; printf '{"severity":"error","path":"BRIEF.md","message":"missing section: %s"}' "$m"; first=0; done; printf ']}'; exit 1; fi; echo '{"summary":{"errors":0,"warnings":0},"findings":[]}'

notes: Defined but not executable. Until execution surface is decided, Station 1 runs /design-brief validate manually before /aesthetic-fanout. This gate fires intra-station between Phase 1 (strategic) and Phase 2 (aesthetic) — semantically before the aesthetic-lock-gate.

name: Aesthetic Lock Gate
description: Asserts that .agentics/AESTHETIC.md exists, was produced by the aesthetic-fanout judge, and has all required sections (signature move, era/mood/archetype tags, anti-pattern blocklist).

args: F=.agentics/AESTHETIC.md; test -f "$F" || { echo '{"summary":{"errors":1},"findings":[{"severity":"error","path":"$F","message":"AESTHETIC.md missing — Station 1 did not run aesthetic-fanout."}]}'; exit 1; }; missing=(); for h in 'The signature move' 'Era / Mood / Archetype tags' 'Typographic direction' 'Layout direction' 'Motion direction' 'Anti-patterns this direction commits to never doing'; do grep -F "## $h" "$F" >/dev/null || missing+=("$h"); done; if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then printf '{"summary":{"errors":%d},"findings":[' ${#missing[@]}; first=1; for m in "${missing[@]}"; do [ $first -eq 1 ] || printf ','; printf '{"severity":"error","path":"AESTHETIC.md","message":"missing section: %s"}' "$m"; first=0; done; printf ']}'; exit 1; fi; echo '{"summary":{"errors":0,"warnings":0},"findings":[]}'

notes: Defined but not executable. Until execution surface is decided, Station 1 attaches AESTHETIC.md verbatim to its report so this gate's check can be done manually.

name: Design System Lint Gate
description: Blocks transition out of the Design System station until DESIGN.md passes @google/design.md lint with zero errors. Warnings are surfaced but do not block.
notes: Defined but not executable. Until execution is wired, Station 02 attaches the lint JSON to its report.

name: DESIGN.md Extended Sections Gate
description: Asserts DESIGN.md contains the four non-normative sections that downstream stations depend on: Voice & Tone, Motion Principles, The Unforgettable Thing, Competitive Differentiators.

args: F=DESIGN.md; test -f "$F" || { echo '{"summary":{"errors":1},"findings":[{"severity":"error","path":"$F","message":"DESIGN.md missing"}]}'; exit 1; }; missing=(); for h in 'Voice & Tone' 'Motion Principles' 'The Unforgettable Thing' 'Competitive Differentiators'; do grep -F "## $h" "$F" >/dev/null || missing+=("$h"); done; if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then printf '{"summary":{"errors":%d},"findings":[' ${#missing[@]}; first=1; for m in "${missing[@]}"; do [ $first -eq 1 ] || printf ','; printf '{"severity":"error","path":"DESIGN.md","message":"missing extended section: %s"}' "$m"; first=0; done; printf ']}'; exit 1; fi; echo '{"summary":{"errors":0,"warnings":0},"findings":[]}'

notes: Defined but not executable. The 4 extended sections are non-normative per the @google/design.md spec, so we enforce them ourselves.

name: Design Brief Owner Approval Gate
description: Asserts every design-brief/*.md has its '[x] Owner approved.' line checked before Station 4 starts implementation.

args: missing=(); for f in design-brief/SIGNATURE-MOVE.md design-brief/LAYOUT.md design-brief/COPY.md design-brief/MOTION.md; do test -f "$f" || { missing+=("$f (missing)"); continue; }; grep -F '[x] Owner approved.' "$f" >/dev/null || missing+=("$f (unchecked)"); done; if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then printf '{"summary":{"errors":%d},"findings":[' ${#missing[@]}; first=1; for m in "${missing[@]}"; do [ $first -eq 1 ] || printf ','; printf '{"severity":"error","path":"%s","message":"brief not approved or missing"}' "$m"; first=0; done; printf ']}'; exit 1; fi; echo '{"summary":{"errors":0,"warnings":0},"findings":[]}'

notes: Defined but not executable. Until execution is wired, Station 4's orchestrator runs this check manually before scaffolding.

name: Adversarial Critique Panel Gate
description: Asserts the 4-persona critique panel ran at least once on the implemented landing page, hit Critic D's automated checks (design-md lint 0 errors, token integrity 0, axe 0, Lighthouse a11y >=95), and either converged or escalated to the project owner. Cap of 3 iterations enforced upstream by the orchestrator.

args: test -f /tmp/critique-final-summary.json || { echo '{"summary":{"errors":1},"findings":[{"severity":"error","path":"/tmp/critique-final-summary.json","message":"critique panel did not run or did not write its summary"}]}'; exit 1; }; cat /tmp/critique-final-summary.json

notes: Defined but not executable. The expected /tmp/critique-final-summary.json schema is documented in the gate-tool-runner spec; Station 4's critique panel writes it. Until execution is wired, Station 4 attaches this file to its report.
